goal: Any visitor can register as an owner with email+password; existing users can request a reset email and set a new password via the link; signed-in users can change their password — all bilingual EL/EN, integrated into the existing auth pattern.
why: Replaces the seeded-account assumption with a real self-service lifecycle — the credibility line for the sales demo. Constraint: no service-role key, so admin.* APIs are forbidden; signup role is stamped by a DB trigger.
success_criteria: /signup renders a bilingual email/password/confirm form; a valid submit creates an auto-confirmed user with app_metadata.role = 'owner' via the Task-1 trigger and redirects to /.
A duplicate-email signup returns an inline bilingual error; mismatched passwords are caught before submission.
/forgot-password submits a registered email, calls resetPasswordForEmail server-side, and shows a bilingual check-your-inbox confirmation.
/auth/reset-password?code=… exchanges the code, lets the user set a new password via changePassword, and redirects to /login.
changePassword exists in account-actions.ts and calls updateUser({ password }) with the current session — no service-role admin call anywhere.
npx tsc --noEmit exits 0 (proves every new i18n key has EL+EN parity); no TODO/FIXME in touched files.

tasks.title: Signup role+confirm trigger migration
acceptance_criteria: An auth.users email-provider row with no role in raw_app_meta_data ends up with raw_app_meta_data->>'role' = 'owner'.
The same row has email_confirmed_at set (non-null) so the new user can sign in immediately.
Only email-provider rows are touched and an existing role is never overwritten (admin/mechanic seeds unaffected).
tasks.action: Create SECURITY DEFINER function public.stamp_signup_owner_role(): when raw_app_meta_data->>'role' is null, set new.raw_app_meta_data via jsonb_set merging provider:email and role:owner, set new.email_confirmed_at := coalesce(new.email_confirmed_at, now()), return new. Attach as a BEFORE INSERT trigger on auth.users so it runs before on_auth_user_created. Operator applies via Supabase MCP after merge; builder writes the .sql only.

tasks.title: Auth lifecycle server actions (signUp, resetPassword, changePassword)
acceptance_criteria: signUp validates email+password(min 8)+confirm-match server-side; duplicate email returns { error: 'exists' }; success redirects to /.
requestPasswordReset validates email and calls resetPasswordForEmail with redirectTo /auth/reset-password; returns { sent: true } on a valid-format email without account enumeration.
changePassword validates new password (min 8)+confirm, calls updateUser({ password }) with the current session, returns { ok: true } or a mapped error.
tasks.action: Create a 'use server' module mirroring auth-actions.ts (Zod parse, await createClient, useActionState state shape, redirect outside try/catch). Export SignUpState, ResetRequestState, ChangePasswordState + initial consts. signUp uses supabase.auth.signUp, mapping user_already_exists to error exists. requestPasswordReset calls resetPasswordForEmail with redirectTo origin+/auth/reset-password. changePassword checks getUser then updateUser. No supabase.auth.admin anywhere.

tasks.title: Signup + forgot-password pages and i18n keys
acceptance_criteria: /signup renders a bilingual email/password/confirm card styled like /login; mismatched passwords caught client-side; duplicate-email shows inline bilingual error; valid submit redirects to /.
/forgot-password renders a single bilingual email field and shows a bilingual confirmation driven by state.sent after submit.
Both pages show LanguageToggle and a link back to /login; /login gains Sign up and Forgot password links.
npx tsc --noEmit passes, proving every new t.* key exists in both el and en.
tasks.action: Add the ~20 auth-lifecycle i18n keys (signup, forgot-password, and T4's reset-completion strings — full list in phase-1-plan.md Task 3) to the Translation type and BOTH el and en dictionaries. Build src/app/signup/page.tsx ('use client', useActionState(signUp)) and src/app/forgot-password/page.tsx (useActionState(requestPasswordReset)) reusing the /login card classes and useLang. Add Sign up + Forgot password links to login/page.tsx using text-accent.

tasks.title: Reset-password completion route
acceptance_criteria: Visiting /auth/reset-password?code=<valid> exchanges the code server-side and renders a bilingual new-password form.
Submitting a valid matching password calls changePassword, succeeds, and redirects to /login.
A missing/invalid code shows a bilingual error with a back-to-login link instead of the form.
A weak password rejected by Supabase surfaces state.error === 'weak' as a bilingual inline message; mismatched passwords caught before submit.
tasks.action: Async Server Component reads searchParams.code; if present, await createClient().auth.exchangeCodeForSession(code); on error render a bilingual invalid-link shell with a back link. On success render a 'use client' ResetPasswordForm using useActionState(changePassword) with new+confirm inputs and a mismatch guard; on state.ok router.push('/login') (keeps changePassword return-based for Phase 2). Reuse /login card classes and t.* strings; semantic tokens only.
